description:
Advances: specled.evidence_store

specled_-7xz (evidence-sync entry tolerance) added a warnings: [%{code:, message:}] field to SpecLedEx.Evidence.Sync.run/2's result map, populated when an entry is quarantined instead of halting sync (invalid path, malformed JSON, unsupported schema_version). The ADMISSION SCOPE for specled_-7xz restricted Allowed touches to lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex, lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex, .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md, and test/specled_ex/evidence/**, so lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex and lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex were intentionally left untouched.

Follow-up: wire result.warnings into both tasks' CLI output, following the existing convention in lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (record_evidence/2, around line 237), which prints Store.record/2's warning tuple via Mix.shell().error(warning.message). Sync.run/2 already returns the data; only the two mix task call sites need updating, plus test coverage in test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs and test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs.

Not a functional gap (sync no longer halts either way) -- purely an operator-visibility improvement so a quarantined entry does not go unnoticed by whoever runs mix spec.sync or mix spec.prune.

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, post-epic follow-up; lands on epic branch specled_-csg/evidence-ledger-split, base a70ed41)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex
- test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (verification stubs only, if needed)
Out of scope (intent): wire result.warnings into both tasks' CLI output following the existing spec.check record_evidence/2 convention (Mix.shell().error(warning.message)). No changes to Sync/Store/Entry behavior — the data path already exists; this is presentation only. Evidence still never gates: warnings must not change any exit status. Any file outside these globs must be disclosed in IMPLEMENTED.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl worker (delegated, specled_-6j7/surface-sync-warnings)
Log: N/A (no separate log capture configured for this delegated run)

Commit: c79a7ae
Files touched: lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs
Tests added:
  - Mix.Tasks.SpecSyncTaskTest "task surfaces a quarantine warning without raising or changing exit status"
  - Mix.Tasks.SpecPruneTaskTest "task surfaces a quarantine warning without raising or changing exit status"
Verification stubs flipped: none — mix_tasks.spec.md's existing tagged_tests stub (execute: true) for specled.tasks.sync_evidence / specled.tasks.prune_evidence already targets these two test files.
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs (5 tests, 0 failures); mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean); mix spec.check --no-run-commands (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0; the one branch-level INFO finding is pre-existing on lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex from the epic baseline, not touched by this diff); mix format --check-formatted on the four touched files (clean).
Final-tier runs: 1
Deferred must requirements: none — ticket scope was presentation-only CLI wiring; no must requirement was omitted.

Summary: Wired SpecLedEx.Evidence.Sync.run/2's result.warnings into both mix spec.sync and mix spec.prune CLI output, printing each warning.message via Mix.shell().error/1, matching spec.check's record_evidence/2 convention. Warnings are purely advisory: neither task's exit status changes, verified by tests that assert :ok while asserting the quarantine warning message is present in the drained shell output. Diff against base a70ed41 touches only the four files named above.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e (verify-6j7 / agent-averify-6j7-ec6855a837477ab9)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e/subagents/agent-averify-6j7-ec6855a837477ab9.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4 (lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (Sync/Store/Entry untouched; no .spec prose change)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1, passed: 1
  - mix_tasks.spec.md command stub (execute: true): `mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs` covering specled.tasks.sync_evidence + specled.tasks.prune_evidence -> 5 tests, 0 failures.
- Requirements coverage:
  - spec.sync surfaces Sync.run/2 warnings (Mix.shell().error(warning.message), spec.check convention): covered by test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs:61 ("task surfaces a quarantine warning..."). Would fail if print_warnings/1 were never called — MUTATION-VERIFIED: replacing the Enum.each body with `:ok` makes the assert message_contains?(messages, "evidence/entry_quarantined") at spec_sync_task_test.exs:71 fail while the info line still prints, so the assertion is load-bearing on the wiring, not the setup. Seams: real-git fixture injection (inject_raw_entry via hash-object/update-index/write-tree/commit-tree/update-ref into refs/heads/spec-evidence) then the real Mix.Tasks.Spec.Sync.run — Sync is NOT mocked.
  - spec.prune surfaces Sync.run/2 warnings: covered by test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs:46. Would fail if print_warnings/1 absent — MUTATION-VERIFIED (fails at spec_prune_task_test.exs:60 with the info line still present). Same real-git injection seam; no Sync mock.
  - exit status unchanged by warnings (evidence never gates): covered — both new tests assert `:ok = ...run(...)`; each task's run/2 returns an explicit `:ok` after print_warnings/1, and print_warnings/1's Enum.each return value is discarded, so warnings cannot alter the return. The best-effort/failure contracts are unchanged (spec.sync failure path untouched).
  - no-op short-circuit (equal SHAs, warnings: []): TRACED — run_attempt returns %{..., warnings: []} before reading any entry; print_warnings([]) = Enum.each over [] prints nothing and cannot crash. Existing spec.sync/spec.prune tests exercising non-warning paths still pass, so the empty-warnings path prints no extra output.
  - spec.prune still works when pruning occurs: existing "explicit prune keeps..." test (spec_prune_task_test.exs:19) untouched and passing; the raw quarantined entry is dropped by the keep-set filter while its warning is still surfaced (generated during entries() read, before keep filtering).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Presentation-only; Sync/Store/Entry unchanged; warning struct %{code, message} consumed exactly as spec.check does. No new spec obligation (sync_evidence/prune_evidence statements do not mention warning surfacing, so no prose co-update owed).
- Targeted verification: PASS.
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
  - mix test on both task files: 5 tests, 0 failures
  - mix test --stale: 3 properties, 1003 tests, 0 failures
  - Worktree restored clean after mutation test (git status --porcelain empty)
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: PASS

Notes: The prune test also asserts the surviving reachable entry (`reachable in evidence_ids(repo)`), a nice extra guard that pruning of the injected raw entry does not disturb the valid one. Both new inject_raw_entry helpers are duplicated verbatim across the two test files; harmless, could be hoisted to test_support later (advisory only).

APPROVED. Merged into specled_-csg/evidence-ledger-split. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: Surface Sync.run/2 quarantine warnings in mix spec.sync and spec.prune output
